The American Revolution was led by colonists who were already the privileged elite and did not extend natural rights to enslaved people; the Haitian Revolution was led by enslaved people who took Enlightenment rights rhetoric at its logical conclusion, abolishing slavery and racial hierarchy entirely.

Industrial capitalism concentrated workers in factories under dangerous conditions with long hours and low wages, while employers had all the bargaining power over individual workers. Collective organization gave workers countervailing power to negotiate better conditions through the threat of collective action (strikes).

industrial capitalism concentrated workers in factories under dangerous conditions with long hours and low wages while employers had all the bargaining power over individual workers collective organization gave workers countervailing power to negotiate better conditions through the threat of collective action

Meiji Japan undertook comprehensive structural transformation — replacing feudalism with capitalism, the samurai class with a conscript army, the shogunate with a constitutional monarchy — while China and the Ottomans attempted selective modernization that preserved traditional power structures, producing more limited results.

meiji japan undertook comprehensive structural transformation - replacing feudalism with capitalism the samurai class with a conscript army the shogunate with a constitutional monarchy - while china and the ottomans attempted selective modernization that preserved traditional power structures producing more limited results

The French Revolution produced universalistic ideological claims (natural rights, popular sovereignty, liberty) that could be applied by any oppressed group, combined with the destabilization of empires during the Napoleonic Wars that created political openings for revolution.
